entry.title: Today's Puzzle: Why is SAP not in the Gartner 2025 FCCS Magic Quadrant?
entry.content:
The Gartner Magic Quadrant for Financial Close and Consolidation (FCCS) was released in March 25, 2025, and, as in years’ past, SAP is not included.

“But surely SAP has a solution for FCCS? Why is SAP not participating?”

Simply put, SAP is not included because SAP is an ERP Suite solution provider, and not a standalone FCCS solution.

Typically, Gartner’s FCCS evaluates solutions that offer a wider range of cloud-based capabilities like financial close management, consolidation, and financial reporting.

SAP customers can benefit from SAP solutions optimized for their data, processes, and landscape:

  • Cloud ERP core capabilities for record to report address the processing steps required to create an entity-level financial report. Data is validated at various points in the process and reconciled by design, with full traceability from the financial report to the underlying financial transaction – and even back to the logistical source – like a sales or purchase order.

This core functionality can be extended if your company needs to manage additional requirements related to multiple geographies, systems, or complex company structures:

  • SAP Advanced Financial Closing is an orchestration hub that coordinates and automates closing tasks in a single-system or multi-system environment. It provides prescriptive automation content for the required closing steps in SAP S/4HANA Cloud – but companies can extend these steps to cover manual tasks or tasks performed in different systems. Additional automation to cover specific media or systems gaps, or very complex workflow, can be added with a no-code / low-code approach using SAP BTP’s Process Automation capabilities.
  • SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting automates the financial consolidation routines required to collect data from all of the entities in the group, manages complex consolidation tasks (currency translations, matrix hierarchies, intercompany eliminations, investment consolidations, etc) and generates financial reports – now with (new!) AI-support for summarization and financial analysis

There are several advantage to using these SAP solutions to meet your FCCS requirements. You can

  • avoid extra reconciliation tasks and risks and delays associated with ETL processes,
  • enforce data quality checks and validations of balances on various financial positions and other managerial dimensions such as cost object, profit center or segment,
  • identify and correct issues at the source – since you’re always working in one environment, you do not have to re-load data when there are adjustments
  • perform tasks before the close – tasks like goods and invoice receipt reconciliation or intercompany can be done on a continuous basis during the period, taking the pressure off the period-end close
  • drill-through auditability for greater visibility and trust – from the highest level consolidated group revenue number, you can drill back to an individual sales order in a specific country,
  • share a user experience across accounting transaction processing, entity close, and group reporting – with the re-use of configurations.

While the vast majority of SAP customers are using these SAP solutions in the SAP Business Suite context, there are some companies that have deployed a “sidecar” approach with AFC and Group Reporting in a cloud system - separate from their financial accounting. This is enabled by SAP Group Reporting’s data loading capabilities (SAP Group Reporting Data Collection ) which allow data to be loaded in a controlled way for entities with accounting data in a separate system.

This setup offers some advantages for specific customer segments, such as immediate access to innovations and SAP’s AI capabilities and roadmap (while their accounting system may be on premise and subject to longer upgrade cycles). However, the advantages that SAP Business Suite integrated-stack customers report in terms of “continuous accounting” and “immediate insight of group-level performance during the period” are not achievable.

Want to learn more? Check out our sap.com pages!

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content:
Thank you, Caetano. No comments. Sharing is caring, and being open is necessary. In 20 years of experience, sometimes I never know about 86 controls of an order type; however, when I land on the screen, I get the power and resolve it fast, so it's all in the learning and keep sharing. Looking forward to working with all. 

Have a good weekend. 

Thank you, 

Sreekanth

entry.title: Re: Edit Relational Connections in CMC for SAP BO BI 4.3
entry.content: Relational connections cannot be modified within the CMC. To make changes, you must use the BO client, either the Universe Design Tool (UDT) or the Information Design Tool (IDT).

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content: I've had this happen on a few occasions too, but at least when it has happened I have been provided more specifics and no sense of urgency.  I do, however, agree that it's better that it be posted in public for the benefit of the entire community.

entry.title: Re: SapUniversalID is Case-Sensitive on the User-Name?!
entry.content: it happen at the SAP Univesal ID sign in-screen: (when I wanted to login to community.sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Create_by_url : Issue in patch call from SAP ABAP
entry.content: Through SICF recorder(Client) i am getting sent url and body contents and In postman <>code i have checked. But i am not able to check header sent from SAP in SICF recorder

entry.title: Manage Supplier Quotations Custom Field/Logic
entry.content: I created a checkbox field at header for manage supplier quotation app in MM_PURDDOC_HEADER business context. After that i created a custom logic that controls the checkbox if it's checked or not in MM_PUR_S4_QTN_CHECK. Even though I have ticked the checkbox in the header, I am getting an error saying that this field is not ticked in the items. What could be the problem?

entry.title: ‌🇧🇷‌ Virtual Session: HCM Brazil - Crédito do Trabalhador (eConsignado)
entry.content:
Are you an SAP customer or partner who would like to learn about SAP's new solution for Crédito do Trabalhador (eConsignado)? Join our online meeting on May 5, 2025!

What to expect?

  • Program Overview: Understand SAP's perspective on this new government program, from credit application to the operationalization of payroll deduction and its impact on eSocial and Digital FGTS.
  • Solution Presentation: Discover how the SAP solution can support employers with this new responsibility, providing efficiency and precision.
  • Demonstration: See the SAP solution in action.
  • Questions and Answers: Take this opportunity to clarify all your questions about the program and the solution presented.

How to register?

  • To register, simply click on this link and complete the necessary information.
  • We'll review your request and send you a confirmation email or notify you if there are any issues.
  • Please note that only registrations using corporate email addresses can be approved.

Please check out the details in the blog post.

entry.title: Re: How can we switch shop floor systems
entry.content: Unfortunately we faced no other option than to recreate everything.

entry.title: #COE#SW01 Change log for custom field
entry.content:
In S4Hana Cloud Public edition we have a custom field on the Business Partner master data. How do I view changes that have been made i.e. which users have entered what data into this field and when?

It doesn't show up in Display Changes to Customers or Maintain Business Partner logs.

entry.title: Re: How to automatically create a Job Information event at a termination?
entry.content: Is it for Hungary-country-specific absence leave “Gardening Leave” ? Or a global need similar to it ?

entry.title: Re: Can I set a retraining date based on item Completion...
entry.content: TracyB, you've got a good point. Just reviewed the documentation on Curricula again and it does seem the better tool. Thanks.

entry.title: How Agentic AI Is Transforming SAP Process Mining
entry.content:
Introduction

Process Mining has emerged as a vital tool in the modern enterprise toolkit—especially for SAP-driven organizations. It allows businesses to visualize and analyze how processes are actually executed, based on real-time system logs rather than assumptions or design blueprints.

Now, with the rise of Agentic AI—autonomous, decision-capable entities—and Generative AI, which enhances insights with contextual intelligence, the field of Process Mining is undergoing a profound shift. These technologies are enabling not only deeper visibility but also proactive and intelligent process improvement.

This article explores how Process Mining works in SAP landscapes and how AI agents are redefining its capabilities.

Understanding SAP Process Mining

At its core, Process Mining involves extracting and analyzing event logs from systems like SAP ERP. These logs contain timestamped records of activities such as sales order creation, goods receipt, or invoice posting. Through analysis, businesses can reconstruct the actual flow of operations—revealing inefficiencies, rework, and variations that may otherwise go unnoticed.

Unlike traditional process analysis methods, Process Mining offers a data-driven view of how work actually happens—not how it was intended to.

Key Components

  1. Event Logs: Digital breadcrumbs from SAP systems, capturing every transaction and user interaction.
  2. Process Discovery: Visualizes the end-to-end “as-is” flow, often revealing unexpected paths and bottlenecks.
  3. Conformance Checking: Compares actual performance to expected or designed workflows, flagging compliance issues.
  4. Process Enhancement: Enables targeted improvements by identifying inefficiencies and process deviations.

In the SAP ecosystem, these logs typically come from modules like MM (Materials Management), SD (Sales and Distribution), or FI (Financial Accounting).

From Traditional to Agentic Process Mining

The introduction of Agentic AI changes the Process Mining landscape in two key ways:

  • Autonomous Agents: These AI entities can monitor business processes continuously, identify deviations, and even trigger corrective workflows—all without human intervention.
  • Context-Aware Insights: With the help of Generative AI, raw log data is transformed into natural language summaries, predictive insights, and simulation-based recommendations.

This means that instead of a process analyst manually reviewing dashboards, an AI agent could proactively inform stakeholders that "invoice posting delays have increased by 18% this week" and recommend remediation actions.

How SAP Process Mining Works – Step by Step

  1. Data Extraction: Pull event logs from SAP systems like S/4HANA or ECC.
  2. Preprocessing: Clean and structure data—removing inconsistencies or incomplete logs.
  3. Process Discovery: Reconstruct and visualize actual process paths.
  4. Diagnostics & Analysis: Identify bottlenecks, rework, delays, and violations.
  5. Recommendations: With GenAI, generate reports, simulate improvements, or forecast outcomes.
  6. Continuous Monitoring: AI agents can be deployed to watch processes in real time, escalating anomalies or enforcing policies.

Use Cases in SAP Environments

Procure-to-Pay (P2P)

Common P2P challenges—such as blocked invoices, approval delays, or vendor issues—can be highlighted through Process Mining. With Agentic AI, the system can go further: suggesting vendor switches, raising procurement alerts, or modifying routing rules autonomously.

Order-to-Cash (O2C)

In O2C scenarios, AI-enhanced mining identifies late deliveries, duplicate orders, or payment delays. Generative AI can simulate alternate fulfillment strategies or customer segmentation-based workflows to improve cycle times.

Quantifiable Benefits

  • Full Transparency: Real-time visualizations of how your SAP processes actually run.
  • Increased Efficiency: Identify and eliminate steps that add no value.
  • Improved Compliance: Detect and respond to deviations from SOPs and policies.
  • Lower Operational Costs: Reduce manual work, rework, and process delays.
  • Faster Decision-Making: Use AI copilots for contextual, real-time recommendations.

The Rise of Continuous, Agent-Led Monitoring

Traditional Process Mining offered snapshots—Agentic AI turns it into a live feed.

Imagine an AI system constantly scanning your SAP business events. When it spots an abnormal delay in goods receipt confirmations across a plant, it not only highlights it but also:

  • Diagnoses the cause (e.g., vendor inconsistency, approval backlog).
  • Suggests possible resolutions.
  • Triggers automated workflows or alerts the responsible stakeholder.

This kind of “intelligent observer” is what Agentic AI enables.

A Subtle Shift: AI in SAP Transformation Tools

Some modern platforms are integrating these capabilities directly into their process intelligence modules. For example, tools like KTern.AI are embedding AI agents that go beyond just mining logs—they diagnose, act, and learn iteratively.

Each of the following analytics becomes significantly more powerful when backed by Generative AI summarization and Agentic task orchestration, which ensure proactive alerts, automated follow-ups, and human-in-the-loop actions:

  1. Business Process Steps: Provides a detailed breakdown of each step involved in a business process, helping to identify areas for improvement.
  2. Average End-to-End (E2E) Process Duration: Calculates the average time taken for a process to complete from start to finish, setting benchmarks and identifying processes that take longer than expected.
  3. Top Business User: Identifies the users who are most actively involved in the business process, aiding in optimizing workload distribution and identifying potential bottlenecks.
  4. Top Process Step: Highlights the most frequently executed step in the process, which can be a target for automation or optimization.
  5. Process Step with the Most Rework: Identifies the process step that requires the most revisions or repetitions, indicating inefficiencies or errors.
  6. Activity Frequency: Shows the number of times each business process activity is performed, helping to understand the workload and identify high-volume tasks.
  7. User Frequency: Indicates the count of process steps each user is involved in, aiding in balancing workloads and ensuring no single user becomes a bottleneck.
  8. Throughput Time vs. Total Waiting Time: Compares the average time tasks take to progress with the total delays experienced at each step, identifying and addressing bottlenecks.
  9. Process Step Rework Count: Counts the total number of times each process step is repeated, signaling potential issues such as incomplete data or errors.
  10. User Rework Count: Shows the cumulative count of rework instances by each user, revealing training needs or workload issues.
  11. Top 20 Cases with Longest Duration: Lists the top 20 process instances that took the longest to complete, revealing patterns or issues that contribute to delays.

 

Final Thoughts

SAP Process Mining has long been a valuable tool for organizations seeking to optimize operations. With Agentic AI and Generative AI in the picture, it is evolving from a diagnostic tool into a proactive, intelligent system that not only reports on your processes—but helps improve them, continuously.

Whether you are an SAP PMO, business analyst, or transformation lead, exploring this new frontier of autonomous process intelligence may be the key to unlocking the next level of operational excellence.

entry.title: Re: How to automate creation of Inbound delivery, GR, WT/...
entry.content:
Hi @Xiawei1 ,

to my knowledge your question refers to Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In order to automate such steps I'd propose to consider the following approach for a custom solution

  1. Utilize the Inbound Delivery Event to be notified of the creation of an inbound delivery
  2. Utilize the Warehouse Inbound Delivery API to post goods receipt
  3. Utilize the Warehouse Order and Task API to create the putaway warehouse tasks

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: Value of custom fields not shown in list FAGLB03
entry.content:
Did you already execute streps from 2890778 - Special fields are not available in T-code FAGLL03 

CALL FUNCTION 'ITEM_STRUC_EXTENSION'
 EXPORTING
   BASIC_STRUCNAME          = 'FAGLPOSY'
   EXT_STRUCNAME            = 'FAGLPOSYEXT'
   EXT_FIELDS_TABNAME       = 'T021S'
   I_LSTCL                  = 'D'
   X_TRANSPORT              = ''.

SUBMIT balvbufdel...

entry.title: Re: How to extend headers in fiori list object
entry.content: Look at this https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/c0eec49db81a441e878f528c8f3d28de

entry.title: Re: Mass Data Management Support For Job Info
entry.content:
Hi @NehaMV13,

This is a very welcome enhancement, but two things would make it even better:

  1. Allow more than 50 records to be updated. This limit is far too low to make this very useful for us.
  2. Allow an option to update the latest sequence Job Info record if one already exists for the effective date selected, instead of adding yet another new record for that same date.

Are there any plans in these two areas for the future?

Regards,
Jay

entry.title: Re: Deployment to separate micro services
entry.content: This statement "without any change in their models or code" is still there. Also no official guide seems to be available and linked in CAPM documentation.

entry.title: Re: Training, practice, free usage of system
entry.content: do you know how we can filter out the subscription based ones to leave only the free ones? I can't seem to find any

entry.title: Re: mail forms attributes for focused build defect manage...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

If you are using the Attribute Context: Focused Build Attributes

Additional Fields > Object ID

This should be the CRM document ID. Then in the mailform body you can add a text label

i.e. Defect ID: [Object ID]

entry.title: Re: Not able to update the values in S4Hana public with P...
entry.content:
Hi Kaito2, - Glad you got it working with Patch!

Could you provide a few more details?

We assume you are using this api: https://api.sap.com/api/CE_PURCHASEORDER_0001/resource/Purchase_Order

correct? (looks like yes from your screen shot). - First thought is you should be using a PATCH and not PUT - i don't see an operation for PUT.

Is the custom field enabled for the API?

If you do a GET immediately after you do the PATCH, is the custom field updated?

I have seen if you are trying to refresh immediately in the UI, it does not look updated right away due to cache. - Could you try to close the app and open it again?

Whereas the GET should show the change immediately. More info about how you are trying to validate the success.

How are you identifying the PO you want to update? It is not clear in the API call you are making and it is not in the body.

I don't think you need the API Key but could be wrong. I assume you got this from the Business Accelerator Try Out feature.

When you mentioned you were successful in the Try Out feature, did you put in your system specifics in the Environments area?

There is an app Odata Error Log or similar name (try just Odata) - might interesting to see if there is any info there.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Generic Delta Extraction using Function Module not working in Replication Flow
entry.content: Sorry, my first post...fix the formatting

entry.title: Re: FF_5 -To upload bank statement-Account number population
entry.content: O acampo AVKON é preenchido com a conta de crédito do cliente, indicada na FD32.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud and what it means for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I have been in many customer discussions over the past weeks since SAP Business Data Cloud was launched on February 13, 2025 as part of the business unleashed event. People always ask similar questions, also during the technology days of the German SAP user group DSAG last week which led me to create this blog to reach a broader audience to clarify regular questions we get and help you find answers to avoid confusion.

 

Where do I find more information about the product and the announcement?

With the announcement there are new pages on our corporate sap.com website as well as a new page on the SAP community with lots of details about the new solution:

📺 Watch the replay of the launch event

🔗 Check out the product page overview on sap.com

🔗 Join the SAP Community for exchange with your peers

 

What is SAP Business Data Cloud and why does it matter?

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a fully managed Software as a Service (SaaS) solution that unifies and governs all SAP data while seamlessly connecting with third-party data. It provides line-of-business leaders with the context needed to make impactful decisions. The solution combines the functionalities of SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Business Warehouse, along with managed SAP Databricks and context aware Insight Apps. It includes curated business-ready data products from SAP line of business applications.

SAP Business Data Cloud is at the core of SAP's Business Suite strategy, which aims to provide a comprehensive view of business operations by integrating applications, data, and AI. It brings together the world’s most comprehensive and mission-critical data with the leading data platform to deliver transformational insights across all lines of business.

This integration helps businesses manage complexity and interconnected processes more effectively, enabling smarter decision-making and optimized business outcomes.

🔗 Discover more details on the eBook online.

📺 For a more detailed view on BDC watch my overview session on YouTube

 

SAP Business Data Cloud specific questions

Which components are part of SAP BDC?

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a fully managed SaaS solution that comprises several components that are integral to its functionality. These components include:

  1. SAP Datasphere: This component is part of the BDC offering, providing capabilities for data modeling and business semantics, data integration and data management, as well as for the definition of custom data products.
  2. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC): SAC is included in BDC to offer advanced analytics, visualization and planning tools.
  3. SAP BW 7.5 / SAP BW/4HANA private cloud: This component is part of the BDC to support data warehousing needs and the path forward for existing on-premises SAP Business Warehouse customers, while being able to leverage the data product generator to create custom data products based on BW data and semantics.
  4. SAP Databricks: This SAP managed component is included to provide data science, data engineering and machine learning / artificial intelligence (ML/AI) capabilities. Also here you will be able to create your custom data products shared via zero copy delta share.
  5. Insight apps and data products: These are prebuilt apps that consume SAP managed and curated data products to provide analytical, process, or domain insights, and access via zero copy delta share.
  6. Data catalog: The data catalog is a central component that supports federated metadata management providing a unified view of metadata from various data sources. This central catalog is complemented by metadata APIs, which facilitate integration with other catalog systems. The catalog is a crucial component for managing and discovering data products within the BDC environment, ensuring that users can efficiently access and utilize the data they need for their business processes.
  7. Business AI with Joule: AI infusion with Joule and its agents leverage the data foundation provided by SAP BDC and its embedded SAP Knowledge Graph technology.
  8. SAP Business Data Cloud cockpit: It is designed to facilitate administrative tasks such as the installation of Data Packages and Insight Apps. This cockpit serves as a centralized platform for managing various components and functionalities within the Business Data Cloud, streamlining the process for users to perform necessary administrative operations efficiently.

These components work together to unify and govern all SAP data while seamlessly connecting with third-party data, providing a comprehensive solution for data and analytics.

 

What is new with data products?

SAP data products are a central feature in SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC), that provide curated and enriched data sets for various analytical and business needs. Data products are not a new concept to SAP and they are a core part of the data marketplace of SAP Datasphere for custom-built data products.

Here are some of the new aspects and functionalities related to data products in BDC:

  1. Curated and Enriched Data: BDC offers SAP managed curated and enriched data products that are designed to provide advanced analytics and cross-analytics capabilities. These data products are intended to help users make smart decisions and optimize business outcomes.
  2. Plug-and-Play on a Harmonized Data Model: SAP data products are delivered out-of-the-box using an always-on experience and following a harmonized data model to provide scalable access without data duplication. This not only includes metadata, but also includes the data of connected customer systems like from SAP S/4HANA PCE.
  3. Integration with Insight Apps: SAP data products in BDC are consumed by Insight Apps, which provide analytical, process, or domain insights. Currently these apps cover analytic models in Datasphere and pre-defined SAC stories, with the intention to expand the app coverage beyond analytical use cases and also leverage pro-code applications built on SAP BTP.
  4. Data Sharing and Consumption: BDC facilitates the sharing of data products between applications, allowing for seamless data integration and consumption leveraging zero copy delta share. This is part of the broader goal to enable easy data sharing and consumption across different platforms and applications avoiding the need for countless data copies, while using the same data for multiple use cases and tools.

 

 

What are these insight apps?

Insight Apps in SAP Business Data Cloud are low code apps designed to deliver tailored insights and actionable recommendations out-of-the-box. They are a collection of pre-built apps that leverage SAP's curated unified semantic models. These apps are integrated with essential business applications to automate critical daily decisions in various areas such as working capital management, sales and distribution, and people analytics.

They contain an SAP managed collection of analytical assets and functionalities to provide value to customers through analytical, process, or domain insights. They can contain data products, semantic models, stories, planning models, AI-powered analytics, and more.

 

 

SAP Datasphere related Questions

What does that mean for existing SAP Datasphere customers?

For existing SAP Datasphere customers this means you are on the right track with your investment in SAP Datasphere. There will be no immediate changes, and existing customers can continue using their current services without interruption.

Over time, existing customers will have the option to transition to the fully managed solution offered by SAP Business Data Cloud. This transition is designed to be simple and ideally seamless, ensuring that customers do not experience any disruption in their services.

The initial release of SAP Business Data Cloud for controlled availability in Q1 2025 is mainly addressing greenfield customers. Later in 2025 existing SAP Datasphere customers can also start adopting SAP Business Data Cloud and bring their existing landscape into the fully managed SaaS experience.

 

Can existing SAP Datasphere systems be used without SAP BDC?

Yes, existing SAP Datasphere systems can continue to be used without transitioning to SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). Customers currently using SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud services can continue their operations without interruption. Over time, they will have the option to transition to the fully managed solution offered by SAP Business Data Cloud, but it is not mandatory. This ensures that existing customers can maintain their current setups and workflows without being forced to migrate to BDC.

 

How can an existing SAP Datasphere system be integrated into SAP BDC?

Existing SAP Datasphere systems can be onboarded into SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) through a service that will be available at a later point in time. This onboarding will allow existing Datasphere customers to transition to BDC, leveraging the new functionalities and services offered by BDC while maintaining their current data and workflows. The onboarding process is designed to be simple and ideally seamless, ensuring that customers do not experience any disruption in their services.

 

Is the SAP BW Bridge of SAP Datasphere still supported in SAP BDC?

Yes, the SAP BW Bridge is still supported in the context of SAP Business Data Cloud. However, the strategic solution for SAP BW customers is to move to SAP BW Cloud, private edition to accelerate adoption of SAP Business Data Cloud. If customers envision a decommissioning of their SAP BW systems and the SAP BW investment can be covered within the scope of SAP BW Bridge, customers can replace the SAP BW 7.5 or SAP BW/4HANA systems with the SAP BW Bridge via a tool-supported conversion. Customers can benefit from the access of S-API extractors and business content similarly to the SAP BW on-premises world, while moving SAP BW investments to SAP BW Bridge. At the same time, customers can adopt data products and insight apps with SAP Business Data Cloud.

 

Roadmap and Learning

In which data centers is SAP BDC available?

SAP Business Data Cloud will initially be available on various AWS data centers. By mid-year, we plan to offer several data centers for Google Cloud and in the second half of 2025 also data centers from Microsoft Azure.

 

What is the roadmap for SAP BDC?

SAP Business Data Cloud was launched with the SAP Business Unleashed virtual event on February 13, 2025, and is commercially available on a controlled availability basis. It is planned to be broadly available in April 2025 (GA). A more detailed roadmap is currently being developed and will be published on the SAP Road Map Explorer.

🔗 Explore our product plans on the Roadmap explorer

 

Are there any learning tutorials available?

To quickly learn more about SAP Business Data Cloud you can enjoy the SAP Business Data Cloud product tour, which is a click through demo to experience SAP Business Data Cloud's most powerful capabilities.

🔗 To get a level deeper you can learn more with tutorials on the learning journey.

entry.title: Re: Connect & Exchange Corner on SAP Learning Hub, student edition

Michal

entry.title: PodTek - O BOOM de oportunidades SAP em 2025
entry.content:
A SAP Community Curitiba vai marcar presença no podTek da Assumtek– e você é nosso convidado!

@SimoneAzevedo  da SAP Community Curitiba estará presente no podTek, juntamente com o Leonardo Assumpção, Karin Anderle e a Fernanda Chaves, em um bate-papo online que promete muita troca de conhecimento, conexões incríveis e, claro, aquele espírito colaborativo que só a nossa comunidade tem!

Quando? 23 de Abril as 20h
Onde? Online – onde você estiver!

Se você respira SAP, inovação e colaboração, esse encontro é pra você.
Vem com a gente fortalecer essa rede!

Clique no link abaixo para saber mais sobre essa live GRATUITA! 

https://lnkd.in/dDZ_AKRe

entry.title: Re: EXIT_SAPLL03A_005 stock removal strategy is overwritt...
entry.content:
Hello @Maestro 

EXIT_SAPLL03A_005 is the correct user-exit. I think you process T_QMAT incorrectly. As per the enhancement documentation, you don't need to change any fields there, you just need to sort the table.

Function module EXIT_SAPLL03A_005 is called up in order to find
appropriate stocks for a given storage type. In contrast to the stock
placement strategy, this module does not find a storage bin, but sets up
and sorts a table (T_QMAT) of the stocks of a material in a storage
type. If the material is changed, Table T_QMAT is refreshed, but with
regard to contents this table belongs to the user exit and must also be
updated by the exit (see EXIT_SAPLL03A_007 and EXIT_SAPLL03A_008). This
user exit can be combined with the stock removal strategies " " and "F".
If the stock removal strategy is " ", the exit operates as described
above. If the stock removal strategy is "F", SAPLL03A sets up the table
T_QMAT so that the user exit only needs to execute the sort. This table
is set up (directly before EXIT_SAPLL03A_005 is called up) whenever the
storage type has not yet been imported into table T_QMAT, that is, if
there is no entry KZTYP = 'X' in T_QMAT for this material and storage
type. The structure of table T_QMAT is compared with respect to the
database stock with the TO items that have already been generated
internally. Items generated internally by other users are not taken
into consideration.

 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content:
Thank you for all your responses.

I guess I'll start responding with a link to this post to the private messages requesting responses.

entry.title: Re: Creating and generate PDF reports in SAP Commerce (Hy...
entry.content:
Thanks for your comment.

I believe Jasper templates are integrated in Hybris OOTB suite. Is that correct?
It would be helpful if there is any documentation on how Hybris and Jasper templates are integrated and how we can make changes in jasper template using jasper studio and how to verify the output.

entry.title: Re: Project Sap Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @RitaPinto ,

Here is the link to a MS Project you are looking for https://support.sap.com/content/dam/SAAP/SAP_Activate/PP_003%20SAP%20S4HC%203SL%20Download.zip. 

I found it by doing a search, but don't ask me the path to find it :-).

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: Enable Background Elements in SAP Onboarding 2.0 (Enhancement ID: 344613)
entry.content: The data collected in background elements in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting can be syncronized directly to the employee profile, potentially eliminating the need to pass it through Onboarding. Check out the help docs for how-to: https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-recruiting/setting-up-and-maintaining-sap-successfactors-recruiting/candidate-profile-element-sm-mapping?q=candidate%20profile%20sync%20to%20employee%20profile&locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Error during ADT Installation because of missing
entry.content: Dear Celine,

entry.title: Re: Error during ADT Installation because of missing
entry.content:
@celinewilliams 

Dear Celine,

thanks for your response. In the meantime it was a simple network error based on a proxy as far I can see.
I assume some oneline respositories were not accessible which led to missing components and thus to dependenncy errors.
I was able to install everything via cellphone hotspot.

Basically the solution is to check your network settings

Cheers
Tamás

entry.title: Re: Question and Answers requests in private messages
entry.content: I bookmarked it so that I could do the same 😀

entry.title: Re: Using Integration Suite with the power of AI
entry.content: Really cool! Will play around with it!

entry.title: SBGRFCMON How to lock a bgRFC Inbound destination which is empty ?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we leverage bgRFC queued inbound scenario and like to test the processing. For this, we like to lock our inbound destination in SBGRFCMON. But as long as there are no deleted/failed queue entries for this destination, it is not shown ... so we cannot lock it.

In SMQS we can just register an inactive queue and all entries are queued unprocessed. Similar functionality seems to miss at bgRFC monitor

Example:
These are our available inbound destinations configured

and the bgRFC monitor shows only the ones with entries but not all.

Any ideas how to lock (stop processing of bgRFC inbound queue entries) for a given destination ?

Thanks & best regards,
  Matthias

entry.content: Hi Wolfang

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!

entry.title: NACHA file is creating a new batch 5 record for each payment
SAP APM Failure Curve Analytics - Training fails because of data set.
entry.content:
Training a Failure Curve Analytic in SAP Asset Performance Management leads to an error:

"The data set you are using does not contain any data. Please check your data set."

How can I fix that error or what do i have to add the mentioned data.

Also as a Sidenote:
I added multiple notifications to the backend. But the training shows that only one notification was considered for it. What conditions do the notifications have to fulfill (e.g. which state)?

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!
entry.content:
Hi @Steven77 
Please check our How to cancel an existing booking for a certification exam? guide.
If you still face issue, please create a case our support team can then assit.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Fiori Adapt UI - make fields greyed out
entry.content:
Hi, when using Adapt UI, is there any way to set value of certain field in search filters and after that make it greyed out? So that users will see to which Plant (or storage, material, etc...) their filters are adapted but are not able to change them? I know this is usually restricted within auth objects in role, but still would like to know. Many thanks

@Jocelyn_Dart it was not covered in the microlearning below so maybe its not possible?

https://learning.sap.com/videos/adapting-the-ui-of-list-report-apps-sap-s-4hana-user-experience

entry.title: Re: Post Incoming payments with an API S/4HANA CLOUD Publ...
entry.content: Hello, I'm in the same situation today, did you manage to find a solution? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Buyer Responsible Question on SRM Topic
entry.content: Hello Ori,

entry.title: Re: How can I schedule my exam????!!!
entry.content:
When you have your universal ID and subscription and it is time to schedule an appointment, goto:

https://sapcertification.questionmark.com/700161/ext/CertificationHub/

Type in your SAP email as user ID. DO NOT enter your SAP password here but press the link "Federated Log in". This will link your universal ID to your session. 

As a result, you will see a different menu on the top. Use this menu to schedule a certificate and you need to come back to this place when doing the actual exam.

You still have to download and install the lock-down browser. After downloading, you may need to right-click on the file and change the properties. There is a tick that needs to be removed (either 'Archive' or 'Block'). Then execute as a system admin.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA for EHS: what is the "factory" field in S...
entry.content: Thank you Michael!

entry.title: What's New in SAP Subscription Billing (Q1 2025)
entry.content:
Greetings, and welcome to our first blog post of the year featuring the new features we've released over Q1 2025 for SAP Subscription Billing. We've been hard at work implementing improvements based on your feedback, and we're thrilled to share these updates with you. Let's dive in!

Copy Rate Plan with Pricing Schemes to Multiple Markets
To kickstart the array of enhancements, we've made it easier to create rate plans for multiple markets. Now, you can copy an existing rate plan with pricing schemes to one or multiple markets for a product. You do this in the product detail page in the Manage Products app. This feature allows you to streamline your processes and create uniformity across your markets.

Intuitive Minimum Value Selection with Suitable Quantity Operator
To make pricing more flexible, users can now pick either the minimum or the maximum value while using the suitable quantity operator. This provides a straightforward way to select the lower value of two quantities, making it easier to define prices for various business scenarios.

Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
We continue to enhance interoperability through tighter integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The Billing Data Analytics and Subscription Analytics apps have been integrated into the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition launchpad and are specifically designed for the Subscription Specialist role. The apps help you understand and optimize the performance of your subscription business.

Consistent Splitting of Bills after Subscription Header Changes
We've upgraded our logic to better reflect changes made to certain subscription header properties in open bills. As a result, you can now easily ensure the consistency of billing data after adjustments are made to subscriptions. Note that these changes will immediately be reflected in open bills while closed bills will remain unaffected. If you're already running SAP Subscription Billing, you can activate this feature in the Manage Business Configuration app under Advance Activation of Features.

Auto-lock Subscription Prices
We understand the importance of safeguarding your subscription prices against list price changes. So, we've implemented the option to lock the subscription prices in line with the list prices for newly created subscriptions. These locked prices will remain unchanged even if the list price is adjusted in the Manage Lookup Table Data app.
This auto-lock option can be configured by assigning overridable fields to rate plan templates. Additionally, you can still update auto-locked prices to current list prices using the Update Prices option in the Manage Subscriptions app.

Support of Sales Commissions with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
To facilitate better financial operations, we now support the internal and external settlement of sales commissions for subscriptions as part of the Subscription Management with Sales Billing process (scope item 57Z). This feature automatically generates settlement documents based on billing data in SAP Subscription Billing, making it easier to manage the settlement of subscription sales.

That wraps up the fantastic new major updates we've brought to SAP Subscription Billing in Q1 2025! For further details on all features, visit the SAP Subscription Billing "What's New" page on SAP Help.
Remember to leave feedback, ask questions in the comment section, or use the tag SAP Subscription Billing in the Q&A section. Don’t forget to follow the tag SAP Subscription Billing to make sure you never miss any of our updates.
Until next time, stay tuned and take care!
Christian

More Information About SAP Subscription Billing

  • What’s New
  • SAP Help Portal Page
  • Feature Overview

entry.title: This file has limited permissions. Error in Fiori
entry.content:
Hello all,

In the Fiori dashboard when I open the ME3N transaction and open any contract to view its pdf version in preview format, I get the following error. 'This file has limited permissions. You may not have access to some features. 

The issue is that the print preview loads correctly, but due to permission settings I am not able to print or save the file. And I dont understand where are these permissions coming for as we have not maintained in the system or anywhere else. If I open the transaction ME3N in the GUI as well and follow the same steps I still get the same permission error.

SAP Fiori #ME3N #PDF

entry.title: Service Request Search not restricted via auth on Sales Org level
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we are using this app to get service requests. It will be used in more sales organizations and we need to restrict request by sales organization. In screen below that request are shown for both 21x and 22x sales organization. Role on the user is set to org 21x in all needed org levels where sales organization appears. After several tracing attemps we found out that authorization object CRM_ORD_OE is responsible for this. However it is only checked when certain service order details need to be displayed - so when clicking on it, user gets "no authorization message". But how to restrict it on complete list so that only orders related to particular sales organization are dispalyed? 

App details below:

Thank you.

entry.title: Where can I start learning about features in supply chain planning in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring Production Planning in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey discusses essential features and requirements for the various steps in a production planning process in SAP S/4HANA. You explore the core features of Demand Management and what the characteristics and benefits are of the various (groups of) planning strategies that SAP S/4HANA has to offer. Make-to-order production, make-to-stock production, and various related features and functions in SAP S/4HANA are discussed. You also execute a predictive material and resource planning (pMRP) scenario, and you work with MRP Live. The topics of demand-driven replenishment (DDR) and multi-step planning with SAP S/4HANA PP/DS are also covered.

Learning objectives
By the end of this learning journey, you will have been introduced to supply chain planning in SAP S/4HANA, where you will have seen and executed the various steps in a typical supply chain planning process. You will have seen various examples of both make-to-order and make-to-stock planning strategies in action. You will also have worked through an example of predictive material and resource planning (pMRP), and of MRP Live. You will have also been introduced to demand-driven replenishment (DDR) and a multi-step planning run in SAP S/4HANA PP/DS.

Prerequisites

  • Exploring Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA Production Planning

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Service Order with External Service Procurement

hi Ahmed, the Account Assignment category R is currently missing in the Delivery customizing. The development team is aware and will create a fix. Please create an incident for this issue.

entry.title: No Freight unit created from Sales order in S/4 HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

we are on S/4 HANA Public cloud TM. From Sales order, FU is not getting created. I have checked, all settings are there like weight , Logistics profile etc.

I want to use program /SCMTMS/CHK_DOC_INT_TM_INTEGR to see what is missing but I cant use since no SE38 in public cloud and Iam even not sure this program exists in Public cloud TM and if yes which app to use to run it or is there any other way to find out why FU is not created ?

 

rgds

sar

entry.title: Re: Adding a Standard field/ custom field to the standard SD Migration cockpit templates.
entry.content: Thanks for the explanation,  Could you please complete step by step how to handle when going with custom fields, looking forward for your help on creating and mapping and updating custom fields, if possible currency fields too

entry.title: Re: Manage Supplier Quotations Custom Field/Logic
entry.content:
Hi ufuk1 - We would need more details about your use case and what you are doing in the custom logic. Is the check box part of the inbound parameters to the custom logic or are you reading it from a CDS View? Are you sure it is coming back as "not checked" or is it coming back as not able to read the field?

Would recommend you had some trace statements to figure out what is going on.

https://blogs.sap.com/2020/06/24/custom-logic-tracing-of-badi-determination-of-workflow-agents-for-p...

 

Check Parameters in BADI

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/c1b80beb50b942aca0525880...

 

How to debug with Trace

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/d84e5f0955844265bc240668...

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/b82a2ea89a0e42908b127e19...

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Connect & Exchange Corner on SAP Learning Hub, student edition
entry.content:
Hi @MiSu,

The overview you provided seems to be related to situations where individual customers can subscribe. As for SAP Learning Hub, student edition, please refer to the following sign-up pages where instructions are provided:

For lecturers: https://learning.sap.com/lecturers
For students: https://learning.sap.com/students

I hope this is helpful.

entry.title: MD73 Assigned Quantity Field
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a requirement to develop a custom report using data from MD73 transaction. However, I am having trouble getting the values for 'Assigned Qty' field.

I can get most of the other fields using BAPI_MATERIAL_STOCK_REQ_LIST or MD_STOCK_REQUIREMENTS_LIST_API, but this field is missing.

Submitting the report SAPMM60X does not work, and I have also tried calling FM 'REQUIREMENTS_ALLOCATION'  using the same input as the standard program, but the results I get are different from the ones in MD73.

Is there any other bapi/method?

Regards,

Tudor

entry.title: Re: #COE#SW01 Copy data from Business Partner custom fiel...
entry.content:
Hi,

Following are the options which you can consider -

1) Utilizing Side by Side extensibility to read the custom field value via whitelisted Business partner related API's, and then updating the standard field with the copied value using the whitelisted Business partner related API's. 

2) Using developer extensibility, create a custom job to read the custom field value and update the standard field utilizing business object interface https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_BUSINESSPARTNERTP_3 

3) Use IRPA, to copy the value from custom field to the standard field. 

 

Regards

Jai Srinivasan

entry.title: Re: Service Order with External Service Procurement
entry.content: Hello Gert, do you know if the planned service performer in the service confirmation can be automatically derived from a preceding document such as the service entry sheet?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cockpit integration suite with internal Serve...
entry.content: It worked when we copy the link and open in private window.

entry.title: Re: No Freight unit created from Sales order in S/4 HANA...
entry.content:
Hi, 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there are no programs equivalent to /SCMTMS/CHK_DOC_INT_TM_INTEGR to see what is missing.

I would recommend you to review the prerequisites and activities required to enable Logistics Integration

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/08069648fa1f42caa0fc1a4ba5a69d6e.html?version=2502.500

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/61e246f4b34c4e1790d8b7651c0b40a8/ed20c6df79004d9aae4249214e05182e.html?version=2502.500

Also make sure that the stock is available for the respective date. Freight units are only built for a sales order if there is a confirmed quantity. 

Regards

Jai Srinivasan

entry.title: Re: No Freight unit created from Sales order in S/4 HANA...
entry.content: yes Bd9 and other things are activated and sales order are relevant for TM based on ship conditions etc.  Now what should I do ?

entry.title: SAP Business Network's response to e-invoicing mandates
entry.content:
The global e-invoicing landscape is undergoing a fundamental shift brought about by the introduction of government mandates across the globe. The introduction of government mandated e-invoicing regulations presents significant challenges for businesses. These regulations, aimed at increasing tax compliance and improving efficiency in financial reporting, require organizations to adopt new systems and workflows for invoice generation, submission, receipt and storage.

The challenge for businesses

Companies operating across multiple jurisdictions face additional complexity, as regulatory requirements vary by country, necessitating customized solutions to address diverse formats, standards, and timelines. Businesses must navigate these challenges while ensuring their operations remain efficient, cost-effective, and aligned with local and international regulatory standards.

Our e-invoice strategy

As an answer to the challenges and customer expectations we have updated our invoice strategy.

Beyond regulatory consistency, SAP Business Network aims to streamline business-to-business collaboration, transforming how our customers work with trading partners and tax authorities across the globe using SAP and partner apps.

We invite you to consult the detailed document on our strategy.

For updates on features and country specific regulations you can also head to

  • the SAP Roadmap Explorer SAP Road Map Explorer
  • and SAP Regulatory change manager Regulatory Change Manager

Is your business ready?

More information will be shared shortly for organizations operating in particular countries and/or as suppliers on SAP Business Network. In the meantime, you can already prepare for the upcoming e-invoicing mandates by going through this handy checklist.

Stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: A python flask app sends the http request failed to i...
Re: Why cant find the data sources of “I_BusinessUserVH” when creat new CDS。
entry.content: Try Supported Capabilities for CDS Views (archive.org)

entry.title: Query Help - Onhand Inventory Date in Warehouse
entry.content:
Hello, 
Can anyone help me with this?

I need a query to show if there is onhand inventory in a warehouse that was put in there greater than 2 weeks.

The customer has warehouse setup for returns, so all returns go in there so they can be quality checked before moving to the main warehouse for shipping.

I need a query to show me any item and inventory that is in there for longer than 2 weeks.

Example:
Item      Date           Qty
ABC1    04/05/25    100
ABC1    03/24/25    200
ABC1    02/20/25    150

I need to have the 3/24 and 02/20 in the query.

I am having problems figuring out what tables to use.  They are using Hana.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network's response to e-invoicing mandates
entry.content: @JacksonsFive

entry.title: Migration Tooling now supporting migration via the pipeline approach
entry.content:
With the latest Cloud Integration update, the SAP Process Orchestration to SAP Integration Suite migration tool now supports the automatic creation of integration artifacts supporting the Pipeline for Cloud Integration.

For an introduction to the migration tool, check out this blog post. For more details about the pipeline for Cloud Integration incl. example implementations, see this blog post.

In the migration tool, you can now decide between two migration approaches: standard vs pipeline. If you select standard, then one integration flow is created based on one integration scenario (an Integrated Configuration Object or a decoupled PI dual stack configuration) in the SAP Process Integration / SAP Process Orchestration system. If you like to run your scenarios using the pipeline for Cloud Integration, multiple integration flows and the corresponding Partner Directory objects are automatically created.

Let me showcase you the pipeline support in the migration tool along two examples, one for a content-based-router scenario and one for a point-to-point scenario.

Migrating a Content-based-router scenario using the pipeline approach

So, we have created a new package where we like to migrate the scenario to. Once we switch to the edit mode, we see a Migrate button to start the migration wizard.

In the wizard, we first need to connect to the SAP Process Orchestration system.

Once done, we select the scenario that we like to migrate, in our case we select an Integrated Configuration of pattern Content-based-router.

On the next screen, the best fit pattern is identified, here we can choose between Content-Based Router and Recipient List. Furthermore, we select the migration approach. In our case Pipeline. Btw, for Pipeline, it doesn't really matter whether you select Content-Based Router or Recipient List for scenario with more than one receiver because here we use the provided generic pipeline steps and hence the generated objects are the same. For the Standard approach it differs.

On the next screen, you can either create mappings that are uploaded to your integration flows or you can create reusable artifacts. In our case, we go for reusable artifacts. Btw, this step and the next are identical with the Standard approach.

The next screen just informs about which mapping objects are created or reused.

On the Scenario screen, you need to maintain a unique scenario name. This scenario name is used for the naming of the created integration flows and Partner Directory entries. Furthermore, you need to maintain the name of the very first JMS queue of the pipeline. By default, it's PIPX01 following our recommendations. So, we assume that you like to use the integrated messaging runtime. If you like to use the fully decoupled pipeline, you need to change to PIPQ01. If you have configured an own queue prefix, then you need to maintain the queue name accordingly.

If you scroll down, you can see that one inbound conversion flow and three receiver flows will be created. Here, you have the option to maintain the xpath conditions just in case that the xpaths haven't been properly resolved from the conditions in the SAP Process Orchestration system. If you like, you can also change the end points of the scenario-specific conversion flow and the outbound flows to your needs.

On the Review screen, select Migrate.

Once, the migration has been successfully carried out, you get a list of all objects created. On the first tab, you can see that 5 integration flows have been created. The first one is the scenario-specific inbound flow. The second flow runs the inbound conversion, in our case it converts the incoming message in JSON format to XML. The last 3 flows are the scenario-specific outbound flows, one for each receiver. From here, you can navigate to the flow models. The naming convention for the integration flow names are defined as follows:

<Your sceneario name>_<pipeline step>_<pipeline type>[_<receiver name>_<interface index>]

On the Partner Directory tab, you see an overview of the partner ID which has been created and its parameters.

The Next Steps provides you information what activities may have to be carried out post migration, like for the pipeline approach the prerequisite is that you have copied the latest pipeline package to your workspace and have deployed the script collection and all the generic flows.

Let's take a look at the created Partner Directory entry. You can see that a partner ID has been created with the scenario name maintained in the wizard. Because we need to run an inbound conversion from JSON to XML, the string parameter InboundConversionEndpoint has been created pointing to the ProcessDirect endpoint of the corresponding scenario-specific flow. If you like, you can add further string parameters to customize the runtime behaviour, like the max number of retries, see Using the Partner Directory in the Pipeline Concept.

On the Alternative Partners tab, you can see that the sender system name and the sender interface is mapped to the scenario. The sender information is actually used to determine the partner ID.

On the Binary Parameters tab, you can see that a binary parameter with ID receiverDetermination has been created. This is the XSLT which is carried out to determine the receivers. Let's download the same.

Here you can see the xpaths which are carried out to determine the receivers and interfaces. Btw, using the migration tool, we only support XSLTs combining receiver and interface determination in one single XSLT to keep it simple, see Special Cases.

Migrating a P2P scenario using the pipeline approach

Now, let's run through a P2P scenarios. The steps are the same like for the case above, so we skip most of the steps here to focus on the difference. Once we have started the wizard, connected to the system, we select a P2P scenario.

This time Point-to-Point Asynchronous has been identified as the fitting pattern. We again select Pipeline as approach. In addition, we can either select or deselect the Idempotent Process at Receiver Side flag. If selected, we add an idempotent process when calling the receiver to identify duplicate messages in case that the receiver is not idempotent.

Here again, we need to maintain a scenario name.

And further below, you can see that we only have one single receiver.

Once migrated, you see that two integration flows have been created, one for the inbound and one for the outbound.

Now, let's take a look at the generated Partner Directory object. For P2P, we only need string parameters. The name of the receiver is stored in the receiverDetermination parameter, the endpoint in the interfaceDetermination_<system name> parameter.

In case that you can't see the pipeline approach option in your tenant, you may need to wait for the upcoming weekend where the rest of the data centers should be updated, so stay tuned, hopefully on coming Monday you can hold of this new feature.

entry.title: How can I insert a video from https://video.sap.com into my blog?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to create a blog and insert a video from https://video.sap.com into it. 
URL: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_0wvg5tt7

I run into an issue and an error message is displayed:

Is it possible to include a video from https://video.sap.com?

If not, which platform should be used instead? Youtube?

Thanks for your answer.

entry.title: Re: Call S4 on Premise API from Build Process automation
entry.content: @Archana

entry.title: Re: From eDocument Cockpit to Manage Electronic Documents: Better user experience, more powerful fea
entry.content: Hi, I am trying to use the new Fiori Manage Electronic Documents application and when I want to view an invoice on the monitor I get the following error, do you have any idea?

entry.title: Re: A Condition in BW Query (BW/4HANA)
entry.content:
Hello Neerajnaik,

You mean to say zero Suppression is not set by default when executed in Analysis where as its applied by default in RSRT. 

 

If this was your question, in AFO you could set it up from Analysis->Display->Enable Properties View. By selecting the Query name in Data Source you will be able to set the value to have zero suppression.

entry.title: Re: MDK - Obtain section name from context in QueryOption...
entry.content:
At this time the QueryOptions and Image Cell OnPress are returning a SectionTableProxy which does not give you a path to get the specific control name.  We have been adding more proxy classes to enable this type of capability as we go. 

As this functionality is currently not available in MDK standard control, I would suggest you to open a feature request here. We'll review it based on its priority.

entry.title: Re: #COE#SW01 Copy data from Business Partner custom fiel...
entry.content: Thanks Jai. Is there anything available within Custom Logic?

entry.title: 🚀 Discover the Future of Transformation, AI, and Customer Support at SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando
entry.content:
From May 19–21, 2025, SAP Sapphire in Orlando promises an exciting lineup of insights, innovations, and real-world stories designed to inspire and empower. Whether you're leading a digital transformation, optimizing support processes, or exploring new technologies or product features, this year’s sessions offer practical strategies and firsthand experience from SAP experts and customers alike.

We’re particularly excited to highlight a curated set of sessions focused on business transformation, AI-powered support, SAP Cloud ALM, clean core journeys, and smooth customer onboarding. You’ll hear from industry leaders, customer voices, and SAP experts on how the integrated toolchain, agentic AI, and cloud innovation are shaping the next generation of enterprise success.

Mark your calendars and plan your visit—these are sessions you won’t want to miss.

📅Highlighted Sessions at SAP Sapphire 2025 – Orlando

Monday, May 19

ASUG Pre-conference |03:00 PM
Streamlining Transformation with SAP’s Integrated Tool Chain | SAP Cloud ALM | SAP LeanIX | SAP Signavio
Anurag Barua, Carsten Heinz, Vijay Prakash

Tuesday, May 20

SER1652 | 11:00am–11:20am
Redefining support with AI-enabled self-service and enhanced productivity
Tarun Luthra

SER1653 | 11:30am–11:50am
Accelerating success: Unlocking value with smooth customer onboarding
Tom McKie

SER1654 | 01:00pm–01:20pm
Maximizing business value with AI-driven support tools
Jens Trotzky

SER1655 | 01:30pm–01:50pm
Achieving a clean core with a RISE with SAP journey
Sören Ruder

SER2698 | 01:30pm–01:50pm
Data-Driven Cloud Journeys: Smarter Strategies for a Seamless Transition
Thorsten Spihlmann, Michael Bradshaw

SER1558 | 02:00pm–02:20pm | Spotlight Session
Thriving in holiday peaks: How AI transforms system resilience
Stefan Steinle, Donavan Marchywka (Carhartt), Roberto Lema (SWGS)

SER2210 | 02:00pm–02:20pm
Preventing disruptions before they happen: Smarter support for sales
Tarun Luthra

SER1656 | 02:30pm–02:50pm
Streamline your RISE with SAP journey with the power of SAP Cloud ALM
Sören Ruder, Tonja Kehrer

SER2114 | 03:00pm–03:20pm
From “A” to “Impact”: Measuring business value with AI
Jens Trotzky

SER2096 | 03:00pm–03:20pm
Panasonic North America: Monitoring custom apps with SAP Cloud ALM
Sören Ruder, Matthew Simoncini (Panasonic)

ASUG ERP2352 P7YQ9LZM7W | 03:00pm–03:40pm
Empower Your Business: Revolutionize SAP S/4HANA with AI and ISLM
Markus Winter, Priti Dhingra

SER2106 | 03:30pm–03:50pm
Using AI and data-driven insights for a smooth customer support experience
Mohammed Ajouz

SER2209 | 04:00pm–04:20pm
Safeguarding operations during the smooth transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Kristin Alexandrova

SER2207 | 04:00pm–04:20pm
Streamlining migration to SAP S/4HANA with lean selective data transition
Thorsten Spihlmann

ASUG BTP2095 RB4YPGQR67 | 04:00pm–04:40pm
Adopt Clean Core with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP BTP Extensions
Tim Steuer, Deepti Patil

ASUG SER2097 W9ZQ5VYWPN | 04:00pm–04:40pm
AI-Powered Support: Preventive Risk Mitigation
Tarun Luthra, Bharath Ramamurthy

SER2208 | 04:30pm–04:50pm
Implementing independent solutions with AI-driven self-service tools
Anna Withum

Wednesday, May 21

ASUG 6B8N67WDBN | 11:30am–12:10pm
Ask us Anything: AI-infused Customer Support from SAP
Geoff Scott, Stefan Steinle

SER2206 | 11:30am–11:50am
Using AI & data-driven insights for seamless customer support
Mohammed Ajouz

ASUG ERP2254 ZVY8YRL9A9 | 01:00pm–01:40pm
Moving from SolMan to SAP Cloud ALM – What You Need To Know!
Benjamin Schneider, Tonja Kehrer

ASUG FIM2255 AVW5RQLZVX | 02:00pm–02:40pm
SAP solutions for quote-to-cash management: get onboard!
Paula McGuire, Johnny De Sousa Miranda

💡Why These Sessions Matter

From accelerating onboarding to redefining support through AI, SAP is offering a future-ready toolbox for customers to innovate, scale, and thrive. This year’s sessions dive deep into real customer stories, best practices, and the technologies powering the clean core, all backed by SAP’s powerful cloud and AI capabilities.

There’s something here for you!

See you in Orlando! Let’s connect, learn, and lead the next wave of transformation together.

entry.title: Re: In Customer invoice Accounting entry Customer line it...

Customer Invoice (Business Transaction 0200)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/c85faa8e702d422daacc0b6b128ed52b.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Web Methode to push invoice attachment to SAP Ariba B&I
entry.content:
Hallo Experts

I need some inputs/help.

There is an new requirement from one of our client that they are expecting to push invoice attachment separately through Web Service Call Method or through API. Need some insight how we can achieve their requirement and your input will help take it further.

Thanks in advance !!

entry.title: Re: AJUSTE SINIEF 13 E 14/24 - Symbolic Return and Symbol...
entry.content:
Olá Rodrigo

Não precisei fazer muitos ajustes no meu processo.

A parte do retorno simbólico é a mais simples, onde utilizei a ordem de retorno standard REB e trabalhei com motivos de ordem para atribuir os textos de retorno corretamente.

Eu tenho uma tabela Z, copiada da J_1BSDNFXV onde considero tb o motivo da ordem e assim, consigo ter os textos necessários do Ajuste Sinief.

Também criei extensões dos CFOPs de Retorno para gerar a descrição "Entrada Simbólica - Ajuste Sinief 14/24" e nos de venda para "Procedimento autorizado pelo Ajuste SINIEF 13/24"

Feito isso o processo do retorno é normal, onde crio a ordem por cópia do documento de faturamento da saída e se for retorno com nf do cliente para refaturamento dele mesmo, menciono na aba de contabilidade no cabeçalho o nro da NF dele e se for retorno para posterior venda para outro cliente, o que vai é a nossa nf de remessa neste campo.

Na venda simbólica, precisei fazer uns ajustes no enhancement pois usei a ordem standard OR que normalmente não permite fazer referências.

1) Na venda simbólica para o mesmo cliente, é necessário mencionar a chave de acesso da primeira NF no XML e meu time fiscal pediu para mencionar tb a NF de Devolução do cliente.

Neste caso eu usei a ordem standard de vendas OR e coloquei a NF de devolução na aba Contabilidade do Cabeçalho e o DOCNUM da primeira NF na aba de Dados do Pedido no nível do Item e assim, mandei para o campo doc original e nfRef as informações da primeira NF:

2) Na venda simbólica para o cliente diferente do primeiro, é necessário mencionar a chave de acesso da primeira NF no XML e também a chave de acesso da nossa nf de Retorno.

Neste caso eu tb usei a ordem standard de vendas OR e coloquei a NF da primeira venda na aba Contabilidade do Cabeçalho e o DOCNUM da NF de retorno na aba de Dados do Pedido no nível do Item e assim, mandei para o campo doc original e nfRef as informações da primeira NF e as duas referências no XML:

Outro ponto importante, é que tanto na nf de retorno simbólico, quanto nas de venda não devem sair dados do transportador, nem de volumes transportados, já que não tem movimentação de mercadoria.

Na nf de venda simbólica para outro cliente, ainda é necessário incluir o primeiro cliente como "Local de Retirada" e para isso aqui usamos um parceiro específico que me abre a tag e leva a informação.

Acho que é isso! 

Espero ter ajudado

Lúcia Mendes

entry.title: Re: SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning: Integration made easy

entry.title: Re: #COE#SW01 Copy data from Business Partner custom fiel...
entry.content: What do you mean by custom logic? The options provided is based on extensibility.

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE HADR - database replication limit
entry.content:
You cannot spread databases between ASE instances with HADR.  The HADR cluster has a primary (active) and companion (standby) as main sites, with a third, optional, standby site for DR - All ASE instances contain copies of the databases added to the HADR cluster.

You should consider the DR ASE a warm standby instance, not a usable instance for the purpose of splitting replication.  It does not participate in any failover operations directly.  The feed of replicated transactions to the DR is switched as part of failover when the active site is switched.

The cluster (2- or 3- node) is treated as a single cluster - replicated databases have a presence in each ASE defined, when added to the HADR system.

Some data from master is replicated (e.g. syslogin information), but only the cluster database and user databases are replicated.  You are correct that other system databases are not replicated (and should not be).  As your database count must include a cluster database, you would still fit the tested limit of 20 databases.

What I can also suggest is that the primary (active) and companion (standby) ASEs be on the same configuration of hardware so the ASEs can be configured similar or identical - this to avoid performance differences if a failover occurs.  To that end, from ASE 16.0 SP04PL02, replication of administration commands (i.e. update statistics) is also supported by the underlying replication servers and is defined using the 'sap_configure_rat' command of the RMA.

Additional information on how to set up a 3rd-node with HADR is available in Adding HADR to an existing ASE instance - SAP Community and Adding a DR node to ASE Always-on for a Custom App... - SAP Community.

As I am not aware of any incompatibility that would invalidate using a newer release of ASE 16.0 higher than SP04PL02, I must also state that any implementation of HADR for a 3rd-party application should be certified by that vendor as there may be operations (e.g. SQL statement replication - 'select into') that may also need to be addressed and accounted for when adding a database to the HADR environment. 

Chris

entry.title: Re: EC Time - Tipps and hints Nr. 5: how to accrual vacation time accounts based on EC time sheet da
entry.content:
Hi Luis,

If I understood your requirement correctly -

1) In time sheet, can you keep a time type: work (work_off) which will appear only when emp has a FREE day?

2) As per blog, in below rule, change as given in screenshot and store in time account type: Vacation.

Hope this will work. Thanks

entry.title: mass change of fixed asset master data in S/4HANA Cloud e.g. costcenter
entry.content:
Hello, 

is there a possibility in S/4HANA Cloud (public) to change in mass fixed asset master data e.g. costcenter, profitcenter like LSMW function in private or on premise?

Thanks for your help. 

BR Jasmin

entry.title: Transport Management in SAP CALM Tool
entry.content:
How can we manage transports management in SAP Cloud ALM?
What is the process for handling transport requests, approvals, and moving them across systems


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Transport Management in SAP CALM Tool
entry.content:
Hi,
You do not mention if you are using on-premise systems and cloud services.  I am guessing on-premise and starting reply with this, but read further if this is not the case:

Blog post: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher) -> KBA 3468539 will connect you to this.  This blog details much about the configuration, required roles/notes, usage and troubleshooting of Change and Deployment Management for on-premise systems.  (There is no build in approval process -> you would need to utilize API to trigger a such a process from your end.)

In case you are looking to find information on accessing cTMS functionality instead, you can check KBA 3358694 Integration between Cloud ALM and cTMS for a link to this blog.

Best regard,
Technical Support
